title_guess: Devon & Cornwall: Kidderminster, Wilsham, Barnstaple, Padstow, Eden Project

pages.text:
Friday 14th October 2011
We set off, Simon, Eve + Tonic (13) to visit Grandma in the Shruberg at Kidderminster. She agreed to move to Sunrise in Edgbaston. We had a glass of wine.
    We set off for the farm. On the way Jeremy telephoned to say that Tatana had died that morning. It's very sad.
    Thomas has started a teacher training course. William is waiting to get liscensed for Samphire. Peter is doing films + has bought an ambassador. John is in the Gambia for 2 weeks in his 2nd year at Swansea + he's bought a glorious red BMA. Charles has just started at Imperial – innovation + entrepreneurship.
    We arrived at the farm + Kate gave us a delicious supper – 4 little beef wellingtons, roast potatoes, beetroot, carrots + parsnips + blackberry + apple crumble. It was very cold in the night.

__BROOKHOUSE FARM__

£80,000 per yr.

Grapes
3000 bottles per year

Apples
800 tons

Muller - ¼  Solaris ¾ acre
Cabinet ¾
2 or 3 bottles per vine  3000 litres
3 tons grapes
19 tons per acre
40 or 60 acres

[map annotation:] Toby &
Ali[?ce]

VINEYARD [arrow pointing to vineyard rows on map]

Jon's [?dome] [?done]

Mike's workshop.

__Saturday 15th October__
      We breakfasted at 8.. Jamie cooked us bacon. Jamie took us round the farm. He took us first to Simon's surprise - a vineyard! White grapes. Eventually 3 tons of juice a year!
      We also saw Mike's workshop - lovely cabins + Josh's dome + Toby + Ali's chestnut plantings barn. Crunchie was leading out the 2 horses to drag wood.
      After a cup of coffee we drove south to Wilsham House B + B, where I am hoping to celebrate my 60th birthday in July 2013. Richard + Jane welcomed us, showed us the farm (which has [?4] double bedrooms) explained about the B + B across the drive (which we couldn't see because it has people in) answered our questions + made suggestions. It's absolutely beautiful.
      Then we drove on past Lorna Doone's Farm to a nearby camp site, near __Oare__ ★ ★ ★
      Lots of girls doing D of E?
      On [the] to Barnstaple. Simon slept from 9 pm - 8 am, his ear + throat hurt + he's very tired, but better after a good sleep. It

captions: Appledore from Instow Quay, North Devon
View from shop where we bought the cart
pages.text:
was warmer in the night.
      __Sunday__ On to Barnstaple where we managed to buy Micron + cycled along the Tarka Trail (Barnstaple → Instow Rd?)
[postcard: Appledore from Instow Quay, North Devon]
View from shop where we bought the cart

just over 12 miles. lovely country side. We passed salt meadows along the estuary. There only flood at very high tides. They're useful for bird life & growing salt lamb when sheep are grazed in the summer. just like Baie de Somme.
   Back to the van & we drove west to near Padstow where the food is supposed to be good. I've just had an unpleasant shower (steeply raked floor, soapy blocked drain, only one hook) to find Simon struggling to light any gas after charging the cylinder. It will be __very__ cold tonight without even a fridge. I got very chilled with all the doors open. No lighting success on anything. We finally walked up to the pub which doesn't serve food on a Sunday. So I had a gin & tonic & Simon had a beer. The people at the next table were nice - Cradley Heath. Simon managed to light the kettle for tea. Then other things lit. Maybe the cylinder was just too full of gas. I slept in my clothes.

We drove to Padstow & caught the ferry across & ambled round the pretty town. There were a nice family on the ferry. The young & their child lived in Vienna, the Grandmother in little Witley. (Her brother has 2 acres of vines & a wedding barn at Shrawley.)
   Lots of tat in the shops but some nice things too. Lots of restaurants owned by Rick Stein. We had fish & chips. Good fast service. Light batter. Plaice over-cooked. Not much better than Aldeburgh but Simon said he didn't feel as ill as usual after f+c

We timed the ferry just right + drove on the the lost Gardens of Heligan. Very pretty £10 entry. Lovely jungly valley. OLd[?fad] about it. Peaceful inshpite of all the people.     BlagyGate    *+*
   On to __Doubletrees Campsite St. Austell__
V. windy. Lovely view. Simon has changed the windscreen wipers + light bulb. The loos + bath showers are good.

__Tuesday__
We reached the Eden Project by 10. We enjoyed it - names of bee plants, renewable fuel - coppiced birch, hazel, willow in small power stations or heating. We had a Mediterranean meal - Portugese stew with mussels - cayenne - best meal of the holiday. The rain forest atmosphere + pepper were good for Simon's nose. The van was parked in the dog shelter for Tonic.
 We returned to the campsite + had a late walk down to the local church. We have been extraordinarily lucky with the rain - mainly at

night, pinging on the metal roof + a couple of sprinkles in the day.
   __Wednesday October 19th__
 We left the lovely campsite at 9.30 for home. Elevenses was at Roadford Lake, a modern reservoir, to feed all the west country. We could have cycled (around) it. A nature reserve in the north near
      We had a good cheese + apple lunch on a field at Puriton. A farmer had put lots of pvcell panels horizontally in a field.
      We have brought home a banana plant, a tea plant + some [?leg] touch-sensitive grass.

[Printed Eden Project leaflet, no handwritten text. Aerial photograph at top showing the Eden Project site with printed labels: "Rainforest Biome", "Covered Biome Entr[ance]", "Mediterranean Bio[me]", "Stage", "Link", "Land train", "Outdoor Biome". Below, printed sections:

__Stage__
The home of our seasonal events programme:
- Den building
- Ice skating
- Concerts (Eden Sessions).

__Outdoor Biome__
Ten years ago this Biome (with the sky for a roof) was a barren landscape, with no soil and no plants. Today find crops that provide your foods, fuels, medicines and materials. Explore our wild places too and consider why we need to conserve them.

__Rainforest Biome__
The largest rainforest in captivity. Climb to the top on the Rainforest Lookout, find out how rainforests keep us alive and how to help conserve them. This Biome is also about trade and connections: find a smallholding which grows cocoa for chocolate, compare the Malaysian back garden to your own. These places are far away but we're connected.

__Link__
(covered Biome [entrance])
This grass-roofed [building] is the entrance [to both] covered Biomes [—] it helps keep in [heat]. You'll find loos, [a] membership de[sk], delicious food [at] Eden Bakery.]

[Printed Eden Project leaflet continued, no handwritten text. __Core__
Our education, arts and events hub. Home to 'the Seed' our massive [sculp]ture, our schools [prog]rammes and a range [of exh]ibitions. Try out th[e] [world]'s largest nutcracke[r] [and] 'services' that [we] provide (includ[ing] [climate] control) and [disco]ver some of [the] [many] ways in which [com]munities can adapt [to] the needs of the 21st century. Delicious food in Jo's Cafe too.]
